2026-04-21T13:23:45.6259330Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T13:23:45.6259659Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T13:23:45.6260050Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T13:23:45.6260314Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T13:23:45.6260516Z [36;1m[0m

Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. This package replaces the `Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI` package. All features, related to `Agents` management were moved to `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents`.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n* The Agents tools were moved to the `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` package.\n* `GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent` cannot be used with `AgentDefinition` and `AgentRecord` as these classes are the part of the `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` package.\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI/CHANGELOG.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}

(SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Release History\n\n## 2.1.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n### Other Changes\n\n## 2.1.0-beta.1 (2026-04-21)\n\n### Features Added\n- Added Toolboxes sample.\n\n## 2.0.0 (2026-04-01)\n\n### Breaking Changes\n- `Insights` were renamed to `ProjectInsights`.\n- `Insight` was renamed to `ProjectsInsight`.\n- `Schedules` were renamed to `ProjectSchedules`.\n- `Schedule` was renamed to `ProjectsSchedule`.\n- `Target` was renamed to `EvaluationTarget`.\n- `Trigger` was renamed to `ScheduleTrigger`.\n- `ScheduleTask` was renamed to `ProjectsScheduleTask`.\n- `Evaluators` client was renamed to `ProjectEvaluators`.\n- `DetailEnum` was renamed to `InternalImageDetailLevel` and made internal.\n- `InputImageContentParamAutoParam` was renamed to `InternalInputImageContentParamAutoParam` and made internal.\n- `Azure.AI.Projects` evaluations and memory operations were moved to `Azure.AI.Projects.Evaluation` and `Azure.AI.Projects.Memory` namespaces respectively.\n- `AIProjectMemoryStoresOperations` were renamed to `AIProjectMemoryStores`.\n- `AgentManifestOptions` class was removed.\n- `EvalResult.Passed` property was renamed to `EvalResult.IsPassed`.\n- `EvalRunResultComparison.Evaluator` property was renamed to `EvalRunResultComparison.EvaluatorName`.\n- `EvalRunResultComparison.Metric` property was renamed to `EvalRunResultComparison.MetricName`.\n- `InsightSummary.Method` property was renamed to `InsightSummary.MethodName`.\n- `RedTeam.NumTurns` property was renamed to `RedTeam.TurnCount`.\n- `RedTeam.SimulationOnly` property was renamed to `RedTeam.IsSimulationOnly`.\n- `TaxonomySubCategory.Enabled` property was renamed to `TaxonomySubCategory.IsEnabled`.\n- `DeleteMemoryStoreResponse.Deleted` property was renamed to `DeleteMemoryStoreResponse.IsDeleted`.\n- `MemoryStoreDefaultOptions.ChatSummaryEnabled` property was renamed to `MemoryStoreDefaultOptions.IsChatSummaryEnabled`.\n- `MemoryStoreDefaultOptions.UserProfileEnabled` property was renamed to `MemoryStoreDefaultOptions.IsUserProfileEnabled`.\n- `MemoryStoreDeleteScopeResponse.Deleted` property was renamed to `MemoryStoreDeleteScopeResponse.IsDeleted`.\n- `AIProjectClient.OpenAI` property was renamed to `AIProjectClient.ProjectOpenAIClient`.\n- `AIProjectClient.Agents` property was renamed to `AIProjectClient.AgentAdministrationClient`.\n- `RecurrenceTrigger.StartTime`, `RecurrenceTrigger.EndTime`, `OneTimeTrigger.TriggerAt`, `CronTrigger.StartTime`, `CronTrigger.EndTime` and `ScheduleRun.TriggerTime` properties type was changed from `string` to `DateTimeOffset`.\n- Remove the `EvaluationScheduleTaskEvalRun` class.\n\n## 2.0.0-beta.2 (2026-03-12)\n\n### Breaking Changes\n* The `Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI` package was renamed to `Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI`\n* The Agents administration was moved to the `Azure.AI.Projects.Agents` package.\n* OpenAI dependency was upgraded to 2.9.1.\n\n## 2.0.0-beta.1 (2026-02-24)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Updated for full `net10` framework compatibility, including removal of `\u003cEnablePreviewFeatures\u003e` flagging.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n**Transitive from Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI**:\n* `ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition` has been merged into `HostedAgentDefinition`\n  * `Image` is now an optional property on `HostedAgentDefinition`; no other net change\n- Tracing: gen_ai.system.instructions event changed to gen_ai.system_instructions attribute.\n- Tracing: provider name change from \"azure.ai.agents\" to \"microsoft.foundry\".\n\n### Sample Updates\n- Added Evaluation sample.\n\n### Known Issues\nAt the time of release, the following operation areas do not yet support the latest API version and will fail. Please temporarily use the previous library version until the new v1 operations are available.\n - Computer use tool\n - Fine tuning\n - Red teams\n - Evaluation\n\n## 1.2.0-beta.5 (2025-12-12)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Updated for transitive compatibility with `OpenAI 2.8.0`, including substantial changes to the `[Experimental]` Responses API\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n* Fixed the uploading of files fore fine tuning.\n\n### Sample Updates\n* Added fine tuning samples.\n\n## 1.2.0-beta.4 (2025-11-17)\n\n- Fixed an issue preventing telemetry from being logged.\n\n## 1.2.0-beta.3 (2025-11-15)\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Addressed an issue that caused paginated responses like conversation items to never terminate when large numbers of items are fetched\n\n## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2025-11-14)\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Addressed an issue with memory store creation failing when not providing `metadata`\n\n## 1.2.0-beta.1 (2025-11-14)\n\n### Other Changes\n* Added `finetuning` samples for operations create, retrieve, list, list events, list checkpoints, cancel, pause and resume. Also, these samples includes various finetuning techniques like Supervised (SFT), Reinforcement (RFT) and Direct performance optimization (DPO).\n\n### Features Added\n\nThis major update brings feature support for Microsoft Foundry Agents Service, integration with the new `Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI` package, expanded evaluations capabilities, insights, red teaming, schedules, and more.\n\n## 1.1.0 (2025-11-03)\n\n### Other Changes\n* Added `files` samples for operations create, delete, list, retrieve and download.\n- Remove `clientRequestId` parameter from `AIProjectConnectionsOperations` and `AIProjectDeploymentsOperations` methods as this value is controlled transparently by `System.ClientModel`.\n- Remove `type` parameter from `AzureAIProjectsModelFactory.BlobReferenceSasCredential` as this is a hardcoded value.\n- Remove `pendingUploadType` parameter from `AzureAIProjectsModelFactory.PendingUploadRequest` and `AzureAIProjectsModelFactory.PendingUploadConfiguration` as this is a hardcoded value.\n\n## 1.0.0 (2025-10-01)\n\n### Features Added\n* Added convenience `CreateOrUpdate` methods for `AIProjectIndex` objects\n\n### Breaking Changes\n* Name changes:\n  * `AssetDeployment` has been renamed to `AIProjectDeployment`\n  * `BlobReference` has been renamed to `AIProjectBlobReference`\n  * `ConnectionProperties` has been renamed to `AIProjectConnection`\n  * `FileDatasetVersion` has been renamed to `FileDataset`\n  * `FolderDatasetVersion` has been renamed to `FolderDataset`\n  * `SasCredential` has been renamed to `BlobReferenceSasCredential`\n  * `SearchIndex` has been renamed to `AIProjectIndex`\n* Removed `GetOpenAIClient` method. Look at Inference samples for how to get the client now.\n* All other `Get*Client` methods have been removed. Use the `AIProjectClient` properties `Connections`, `Datasets`, `Deployments`, and `Indexes` instead.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n* Properly handle secret key population for `AIProjectConnectionCustomCredential`, [see GitHub issue 52355](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/52355).\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.11 (2025-08-20)\n\n### Features Added\n* Added a constructor for `AIProjectClient` which takes a `System.ClientModel.AuthenticationTokenProvider` object for authentication. We will be switching away from `Azure.Core.TokenCredential` with the upcoming stable release.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n* Class changes:\n  * `AIDeployment` has been renamed `AssetDeployment`\n  * `AssetCredentialResponse` has been renamed `DatasetCredential`\n  * `DatasetIndex` has been renamed `SearchIndex`\n  * `PendingUploadRequest` has been renamed `PendingUploadConfiguration`\n  * `PendingUploadResponse` has been renamed `PendingUploadResult`\n* In `Datasets`, methods `PendingUpload` and `PendingUploadAsync`, argument `body` was replaced with `configuration`\n* `GetAzureOpenAIChatClient` and `GetAzureOpenAIEmbeddingClient` methods have been removed and replaced with a single `GetOpenAIClient` method. This method returns an OpenAI client which has properties for accessing individual operation clients. More information is available in the `Inference` samples.\n* All operations methods have been renamed to include the object the operation is for. For example, `Connections.GetDefault` has been renamed to `Connections.GetDefaultConnection`, and `Datasets.Get` has been renamed to `Datasets.GetDatasets`.\n* `Deployments.GetModelDeployment` and `Deployments.GetModelDeploymentAsync` methods have been removed. Use `Deployments.GetDeployment` and `Deployments.GetDeploymentAsync` instead.`\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.10 (2025-07-11)\n\n### Features Added\n* Support for generating embeddings through Azure OpenAI using `GetAzureOpenAIEmbeddingClient`.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n* Name changes:\n  * In `Datasets` methods `PendingUpload` and `PendingUploadAsync`, argument `body` was replaced with `pendingUploadRequest`\n  * `Connection` has been renamed `ConnectionProperties`\n  * `Deployment` has been renamed `AIDeployment`\n  * `Index` has been renamed `DatasetIndex`\n  * `SasCredential` has been renamed `BlobReferenceSasCredential`\n  * `Sku` has been renamed `ModelDeploymentSku`\n* Removing `GetChatCompletionsClient`, `GetEmbeddingsClient`, and `GetImageEmbeddingsClient` methods from `AIProjectClient`. The Inference client should be used directly instead.\n* Replacing `GetConnectionsClient`, `GetDatasetsClient`, `GetDeploymentsClient`, and`GetIndexesClient` with `Connections`, `Datasets`, `Deployments`, and `Indexes` properties.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n* Fix getting model deployed on the Azure Open AI resource, if the resource is authenticated using Entra ID. [see GitHub issue 49064](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/49064)\n* Fix dataset uploading datasets.\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.9 (2025-05-16)\n\n### Features Added\n* `Deployments` methods to enumerate AI models deployed to your AI Foundry Project.\n* `Datasets` methods to upload documents and reference them. To be used with Evaluations.\n* `Indexes` methods to handle Search Indexes.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n* Azure AI Foundry Project endpoint is now required to construct the `AIProjectClient`. It has the form\n`https://\u003cyour-ai-services-account-name\u003e.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/\u003cyour-project-name\u003e`. Find it in your AI Foundry Project Overview page. Support for project connection string and hub-based projects has been discontinued. We recommend creating a new Azure AI Foundry resource utilizing project endpoint. If this is not possible, please pin the version of `Azure.AI.Projects` to version `1.0.0-beta.8` or earlier.\n* Agents are now implemented in a separate package `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`. Use the `GetPersistentAgentsClient` method on the\n`AIProjectsClient` to create, run and delete agents. However there have been some breaking changes in these operations. See [Agents package document and samples](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent) for more details.\n* Several changes to the `Connections` methods, including the response object (now it is simply called `Connection`). The class `ConnectionProperties` was renamed to `Connection`, and its properties have changed.\n* `GetAzureOpenAIChatClient` now supports returning an authenticated `AzureOpenAI` ChatClient to be used with\nAI models deployed to the Project's AI Services.\n* The method `UploadFileRequest` on `AIProjectClient` had been removed, use `UploadFile` in `Datasets` instead.\n* Property `scope` on `AIProjectClient` is removed.\n* Evaluator Ids are available using the class `EvaluatorIDs` and no longer require `Azure.AI.Evaluation` package to be installed.\n* Property `Id` on Evaluation is replaced with `name`.\n* Please see the [agents migration guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects/AGENTS_MIGRATION_GUIDE.md) on how to use the new `azure-ai-projects` with `azure-ai-agents` package.\n\n### Sample Updates\n* All samples have been updated. New ones have been added for Deployments, Datasets, and Indexes.\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.8 (2025-04-23)\n\n### Sample Updates\n* New sample added for connected agent tool.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n* Fix for filtering of messages by run ID [see GitHub issue 49513](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/49513).\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.7 (2025-04-18)\n\n### Features Added\n* Added image input support for agents create message\n* Added list threads support for agents\n\n### Sample Updates\n* New samples added for image input from url and file.\n* New Fabric tool sample for agents\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.6 (2025-03-28)\n\n### Features Added\n* Added `QueryType` parameter to `AISearchIndexResource` to allow different search types performed by an agent [issue](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/49069).\n* Added sample for Azure AI Search in streaming scenarios [issue](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/49069).\n\n### Breaking Changes\n* `MicrosoftFabricToolDefinition` constructor parameter was renamed from `fabricAiskill` to `fabricDataagent`, the corresponding parameter was renamed from `FabricAiskill` to `FabricDataagent`.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n* Fixed Azure AI Search in streaming scenarios.\n\n### Sample updates\n* Added documentation for each sample.\n* The unnecessary code was
